Off Calendar Oct-13-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT SHAHAN DEREK SOGHIKIAN'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: Rodney C. Lee, Esq. of Ervin Cohen & Jessup LLP 310/281-6367 for pltf & opposing party; Anna Erickson White, Esq. of Morrison Foerster 268-7682 for defts Jean Valette & Nils Lind; Patrick E. Gibbs, Esq. of Latham & Watkins LLP 650/463-4696 for deft Shahan Soghikian; Bess Kennedy, Esq. of Bingham McCutchen LLP 393-2274 for deft William Timken.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified: SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION (FRAUDULENT MISREPRESENTATION) PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE DEFENDANTS KNEW THEY WERE FALSE. PLAINTIFF MUST PARTICULARLY IDENTIFY THE ALLEGED FALSE REPRESENTATIONS THAT FORM THE BASIS OF THE 1ST, 2ND AND 3RD CAUSES OF ACTION. AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION (CONVERSION) PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE THE DEFENDANTS' INDIVIDUAL PARTICIPATION. AS TO THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION (INDUCING BREACH OF CONTRACT) PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD A THEORY NOT BARRED BY SHOEMAKER V. MYERS (1990) 52 CAL.3D 1. AS TO THE 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION (BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY) CORPORATIONS GENERALLY OWE NO FIDUCIARY DUTIES TO CREDITORS. DELAWARE LAW APPLIES TO THIS AND THE 8TH CAUSE OF ACTION (NEGLIGENT SUPERVISION) SEE STATE FARM V. SUPERIOR COURT (2003) 114 CAL.APP.4TH 434. DELAWARE LAW SHIELDS DIRECTORS FROM LIABILITY FOR A BREACH OF THE DUTY OF CARE UNLESS IT IS ALSO A BREACH OF THE DUTY OF LOYALTY UNDER SECTION 102(b)(7) OF THE DELAWARE GENERAL CORPORATE LAW. THE 10TH CAUSE OF ACTION (UCL) DOES NOT ALLEGE THE DEFENDANTS' PERSONAL PARTICIPATION IN THE UNLAWFUL PRACTICES. Notice waived. Plaintiff to file amended complaint 20 days from today. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
